items.title: Het atypische seizoen van de vier centrumverdedigers van PSV
items.summary: PSV dendert af op het kampioenschap en speelt morgenavond in de halve finale van het KNVB-bekertoernooi tegen NEC. Maar zowel in de spits (door blessures) als centraal achterin heeft Peter Bosz nooit aan dezelfde invulling vastgehouden. Jerdy Schouten, Yarek Gasiorowski, Armando Obispo en Ryan Flamingo hebben dit seizoen allemaal hun eigen opvallende verhaal.

items.title: Timber oogst in gewonnen derby Marseille complimenten en zorgen
items.summary: Habib Beye heeft zijn eerste succes beleefd als trainer van Olympique Marseille . Tegen Olympique Lyon leek er niet meer in te zitten dan een 2-2 gelijkspel, maar Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang scoorde in blessuretijd alsnog. Beye was na afloop bijzonder opgelucht.

items.title: Klap na klap voor Borussia Dortmund: einde seizoen Emre Can
items.summary: Borussia Dortmund moet het maandenlang stellen zonder Emre Can. De aanvoerder van de Bundesliga-club heeft in de topper tegen Bayern München de kruisband in zijn linkerknie gescheurd en staat voor een lange revalidatie.

items.title: From Melbourne to Milano Cortina: Robson's curling prep and 19 days without tennis!
‘All bets are off!’ – why F1's new era set for 'wild' season start
items.summary: Formula 1's new era of regulations finally gets under way for real in Melbourne from Friday as the Australian Grand Prix opens a 24-race season in which no one yet seems quite sure what to expect.

items.title: Southwell and Wolverhampton host Monday racing
items.summary: Southwell hosts jumps action on Monday afternoon, before attention switches to all-weather racing from Wolverhampton.

items.title: Robin van Persie, hoelang nog?
items.summary: De aanstelling van Kees van Wonderen als nieuwe technisch directeur van Feyenoord laat langer op zich wachten dan gedacht. Een persbericht werd intern voor vorige week dinsdag aangekondigd, maar vijf dagen later was er voor het duel met FC Twente nog steeds geen witte rook. Als Van Wonderen het duel in De Grolsch Veste heeft gezien, weet hij dat hij ten minste één harde beslissing zal moeten nemen. Áls hij die bevoegdheid krijgt.

items.title: Sprookje voor Insigne: eerste goal in 5041 dagen voor zijn jeugdliefde
items.summary: Lorenzo Insigne heeft in Pescara een cirkel rond gemaakt die bijna veertien jaar open was. De inmiddels 34-jarige aanvaller draagt tegenwoordig weer het shirt van zijn jeugdliefde Delfino Pescara en was van cruciale waarde in de 2-1 zege op Palermo.

items.title: Mourinho: 'Over' for Prestianni if guilty of abuse
items.summary: Jose Mourinho said Gianluca Prestianni will have no future at Benfica if he is found guilty of racially abusing Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: United got 'Old Trafford bonus' in win - Palace boss
items.summary: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner has branded the decision to award Manchester United a penalty in their 2-1 defeat on Sunday an "Old Trafford bonus."

items.title: Messi's first goals of season inspire big Miami rally
items.summary: Lionel Messi scored twice in a four-goal second half, defender Telasco Segovia added a goal and two assists and defending champion Inter Miami CF rallied to beat Orlando City SC 4-2 on Sunday night.

items.title: Iran federation says participation in WC in doubt
items.summary: The president of Iran's soccer federation says he does not know if the national team can play World Cup matches in the United States following the surprise U.S. and Israeli bombardment of his country.

items.title: Arteta: 'Heart almost stopped' on late Raya save
items.summary: Mikel Arteta hailed David Raya's performance in Arsenal's 2-1 win against Chelsea after saying his "heart almost stopped" before the goalkeeper made a crucial stoppage time save to keep the Gunners in pole position in the Premier League title race.

items.title: Bradford inflict first loss of season on Toulouse
items.summary: Watch highlights from the Super League game between Bradford Bulls and Toulouse.

items.title: 'Ugly' or 'beautiful'? Set-pieces prove decisive for Arsenal again
items.summary: Arsenal move five points clear in the Premier League title race but is their reliance on set-pieces an issue?

items.title: Is this really the beautiful game? Well yes, and no … but the panic is fun to watch | Barney Ronay
items.summary: If every win is going to be painful from here, you may as well just take the painful wins – welcome to Arsenal’s late title stagger On Thursday night at a swanky London hotel so luxuriously risk‑averse the toilets are equipped with wireless thermostats to control to within half a degree the heat of the seat, the Premier League chief executive, Richard Masters, spoke in detail for the first time about the prospect of “Premflix”, the direct‑to‑consumer model of the future, an app that will sluice this irresistible footballing opiate directly into the eyeballs of 8 billion rapt humans. In doing so Masters was echoing the words of Todd Boehly on the same stage 12 months earlier, who had talked about the Premier League as a kind of fire stolen from the gods, source of the next great tech platform, an engine of empire, tool of world domination, of lassoing the moon out of the sky. Continue reading...

items.title: Infantino suggests players covering mouths when addressing opponents could be sent off
items.summary: Fifa president wants more intervention in battle against racism Mouth covering in focus after Vinícius Júnior’s allegation of abuse The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has suggested players who cover their mouths while addressing opponents could be sent off as part of the governing body’s battle against racism. The practice, which has long been deployed to prevent cameras picking up conversations between teammates and opposition, has been put in focus after Vinícius Júnior’s allegations of discriminatory abuse by Gianluca Prestianni. The Benfica player denies doing so but was suspended for his side’s Champions League playoff second leg against Vinícius’s Real Madrid pending the outcome of a formal investigation. Continue reading...

items.title: Would it be bigger gamble not to give Carrick Man Utd job?
items.summary: With six wins and a draw from seven games in charge of Manchester United, would it prove more of a gamble not to give Michael Carrick the head coach position full-time?

items.title: Iran, the US, and a World Cup that starts in three months
items.summary: What does the US conflict with Iran mean for what was already set to be a highly politicised World Cup this summer?

items.title: Iranian football association unsure if national team will play at World Cup in US
items.summary: ‘We cannot look forward with hope,’ says FFIRI president England Lions match in Abu Dhabi cancelled due to war The president of Iran’s football federation says he does not know if the national team can play World Cup matches in the US after the US and Israeli bombardment of the country. “What is certain is that after this attack, we cannot be expected to look forward to the World Cup with hope,” Mehdi Taj said to the sports portal Varzesh3 as Iran traded strikes with Israel as part of a widening war prompted by the bombardment. Continue reading...
